title: Concourse Airport Food & Dining — JFK, LGA, EWR, RDU
description: Full list of food and restaurant options at JFK, LGA, EWR, and RDU. Each vendor includes: name, cuisine, zoneId, dietaryTags, tags, avgWaitMinutes, opinion, priceRange (budget|mid|splurge), serviceType (quick-serve|sit-down|bar|food-hall), mealTypes (breakfast|lunch|dinner|anytime), cuisineCategories (american, italian, mexican, asian, coffee, bakery, etc.), shortDescription. Use for RAG and filtering by user preferences.

terminals.description: JFK Terminal 4 is the main international hub with the widest dining range at JFK.

vendors.name: Shake Shack
vendors.opinion: Best burger in T4. The ShackBurger is worth every second.
vendors.shortDescription: Fast-casual burgers and shakes near Gate B14; vegetarian options.

vendors.name: Panda Express
vendors.opinion: Solid and reliable. Orange chicken hits different when you're in a rush.
vendors.shortDescription: Quick-serve Chinese in the food court; fast and filling.

vendors.name: Starbucks
vendors.opinion: Predictable and fast. Skip if the line is out the door — try Peet's or Flatiron instead.
vendors.shortDescription: Coffee and pastries; vegetarian and vegan options.

vendors.name: Blue Ribbon Sushi
vendors.opinion: Excellent sushi for an airport. Worth it if you have 25+ minutes.
vendors.shortDescription: Sit-down sushi near B22; vegetarian and gluten-free options.

vendors.name: Five Borough Food Hall
vendors.opinion: Food-truck style variety. Good when you can't agree on one cuisine.
vendors.shortDescription: Food hall with multiple vendors; good for groups.

vendors.name: Pizza Vino
vendors.opinion: Neapolitan-style pizza. Skip the generic slices elsewhere — this is the one.
vendors.shortDescription: Neapolitan pizza and Italian near B22.

vendors.name: McDonald's
vendors.opinion: Open late. When nothing else is open or you need something fast and familiar.
vendors.shortDescription: Fast food; open late near B32.

vendors.name: Dunkin'
vendors.opinion: 24 hours at B32. Coffee and a sandwich in a pinch.
vendors.shortDescription: 24-hour coffee and donuts at B32.

vendors.name: Tigin Irish Pub
vendors.opinion: Great fish and chips. It's a hike — only if you have 40+ minutes.
vendors.shortDescription: Irish pub with fish and chips and full bar; longer walk to B36.

vendors.name: The Palm Bar & Grille
vendors.opinion: Splurge spot. Save it for a long layover.
vendors.shortDescription: Upscale steakhouse; save for long layovers.

terminals.tips: Terminal 4 has the best food at JFK. Skip the pizza at B14 — walk to Shake Shack. Five Borough Food Hall is great for groups with different tastes.

terminals.description: LaGuardia Terminal B has the most dining options after the rebuild, including Capital One Landing.

vendors.name: Shake Shack
vendors.opinion: Same reliable Shack experience. Always a safe bet.
vendors.shortDescription: Fast-casual burgers and shakes in central concourse.

vendors.name: Capital One Landing
vendors.opinion: José Andrés spot — Spanish tapas and cocktails. Worth it for a longer wait; same-day boarding pass required.
vendors.shortDescription: Premium Spanish tapas and cocktails; same-day boarding pass required.

vendors.name: Mulberry Street
vendors.opinion: Solid Italian. Pasta and wine when you have time.
vendors.shortDescription: Sit-down Italian pasta and wine near B20.

vendors.name: Zaro's Family Bakery
vendors.opinion: 24 hours. Bagels, pastries, and sandwiches. My go-to for early flights.
vendors.shortDescription: 24-hour bakery; bagels, pastries, sandwiches.

vendors.name: Five Guys
vendors.opinion: Custom burgers and fries. Consistently good.
vendors.shortDescription: Build-your-own burgers and fries near B20.

vendors.name: Taim
vendors.opinion: Best falafel at LGA. Quick and fresh.
vendors.shortDescription: Falafel and Middle Eastern; vegetarian and vegan options.

vendors.name: Au Bon Pain
vendors.opinion: Soups, sandwiches, and coffee. Reliable when you're near the B50s.
vendors.shortDescription: Cafe with soups, sandwiches, and coffee near B50.

vendors.name: Dunkin'
vendors.opinion: Coffee and a quick bite. Everywhere for a reason.
vendors.shortDescription: Coffee, donuts, and breakfast sandwiches.

terminals.tips: Terminal B is where the food is. Zaro's is open 24/7. Capital One Landing is the splurge; Shake Shack and Taim are the reliable quick wins.

terminals.description: LaGuardia Terminal C has a smaller but solid set of options, including Artichoke Pizza.

vendors.name: Artichoke Basille's Pizza
vendors.opinion: NYC institution. The artichoke slice is legendary — get it.
vendors.shortDescription: NYC institution; artichoke slice and pizza.

vendors.name: Cotto
vendors.opinion: Italian trattoria with a wine bar. Good for a sit-down before a flight.
vendors.shortDescription: Italian trattoria with wine bar in Terminal C.

vendors.name: Irving Farm Coffee Roasters
vendors.opinion: Serious coffee. Skip the chain if you're a coffee person.
vendors.shortDescription: Specialty coffee near C10.

vendors.name: Jamba
vendors.opinion: Smoothies and bowls. Healthy option when you want something light.
vendors.shortDescription: Smoothies and bowls; vegetarian and vegan.

terminals.tips: Terminal C is compact. Artichoke Pizza is the must-try. Cotto if you have time for a proper meal.

terminals.description: Newark Terminal A has a wide mix of quick-serve and sit-down options including Shake Shack, Guy Fieri's Flavortown, and Ess-A-Bagel.

vendors.name: Bang Cookies
vendors.opinion: Giant cookies from a Jersey City sweets shop. Perfect for a quick treat.
vendors.shortDescription: Giant cookies from a Jersey City sweets shop. Central Plaza.

vendors.name: Bluestone Lane Coffee
vendors.opinion: Australian chain. Solid coffee and tea near Gates 5 and 6.
vendors.shortDescription: Coffee and tea from an Australian chain. South Pier, Gates 5 and 6.

vendors.name: Burger Fi
vendors.opinion: Florida-based chain with burgers, hot dogs, chicken, fries, and onion rings. Reliable and fast.
vendors.shortDescription: Burgers, hot dogs, chicken, fries. Central Plaza East.

vendors.name: Carlito's Barbecue Taqueria
vendors.opinion: Tacos, sides, plates, and churros. Good combo near Gate 15.
vendors.shortDescription: Tacos, sides, plates, and churros. Hammerhead near Gate 15.

vendors.name: City Rub
vendors.opinion: Off-shoot of a Newark sports bar. Known for brisket and pulled pork tacos.
vendors.shortDescription: Brisket and pulled pork tacos from a Newark sports bar. East Pier.

vendors.name: D'Taco Joint
vendors.opinion: Tacos, breakfast burritos, and bowls. Fast option in the food court.
vendors.shortDescription: Tacos, breakfast burritos, and bowls. Central Plaza Food Court.

vendors.name: Dunkin'
vendors.opinion: Coffee and doughnuts. East Pier near Gate 23.
vendors.shortDescription: Coffee and doughnuts. East Pier, Gate 23.

vendors.name: Ess-A-Bagel
vendors.opinion: New York-based chain. Bagels, spreads, and sandwiches. Worth the stop.
vendors.shortDescription: Bagels, spreads, and sandwiches. Hammerhead, Gate 15.

vendors.name: Felina Cucina
vendors.opinion: Sit-down Italian with cocktails and pizza. Good for a real meal near Gates 7 and 8.
vendors.shortDescription: Sit-down Italian with cocktails and pizza. Central Plaza, Gates 7 and 8.

vendors.name: Guy Fieri's Flavortown Kitchen and Bar
vendors.opinion: The Mayor of Flavortown. Wings, pulled pork sandwiches, and trash can nachos. Fun spot.
vendors.shortDescription: Guy Fieri's spot with wings, pulled pork, trash can nachos. North Pier.

vendors.name: Jersey & Co. Gelato
vendors.opinion: Off-shoot of a Jersey City gelato shop. Sweet finish before your flight.
vendors.shortDescription: Gelato from a Jersey City shop. Central Plaza.

vendors.name: Jersey Mike's
vendors.opinion: Subs and sandwiches. Reliable and quick.
vendors.shortDescription: Sandwiches. Central Plaza.

vendors.name: Kitchen Step
vendors.opinion: Offshoot of a popular Jersey City restaurant from chef Ryan DePersio. Hammerhead Central.
vendors.shortDescription: Jersey City chef Ryan DePersio's spot. Hammerhead Central.

vendors.name: Mediterranean Grill
vendors.opinion: Affordable falafel and chicken shawarma. Vegan options available.
vendors.shortDescription: Falafel and chicken shawarma with vegan options. Central Plaza Food Court.

vendors.name: Playa Bowls
vendors.opinion: New Jersey-based chain. Smoothies and juices. Healthy and quick.
vendors.shortDescription: Smoothies and juices from a NJ chain. Central Plaza Food Court.

vendors.name: Shake Shack
vendors.opinion: Danny Meyer's burger chain. Consistently good. South Pier.
vendors.shortDescription: Burgers and shakes. South Pier.

vendors.name: Smokehouse BBQ
vendors.opinion: Burgers, brisket, and sides. Note: caught mixed reviews. Central Plaza near Gates 26 and 27.
vendors.shortDescription: Burgers, brisket, and sides. Central Plaza, Gates 26 and 27.

vendors.name: Sora Japanese Cuisine & Sushi Bar
vendors.opinion: Casual sushi counter with rolls, ramen, and burgers. Food court option.
vendors.shortDescription: Sushi, ramen, and burgers. Central Plaza Food Court.

vendors.name: Starbucks
vendors.opinion: One location in Terminal A. Central Plaza North.
vendors.shortDescription: Coffee and pastries. Central Plaza North.

vendors.name: The Office Tavern Grill
vendors.opinion: Sit-down tavern with several NJ locations. Buffalo wings and burgers. Central Plaza South.
vendors.shortDescription: Tavern with wings and burgers. Central Plaza South.

vendors.name: Tonnie's Minis
vendors.opinion: Offshoot of a Newark bakery. Cupcakes in a cup. Sweet and quick.
vendors.shortDescription: Cupcakes in a cup from a Newark bakery. Central Plaza.

vendors.name: Town Bar + Kitchen
vendors.opinion: Bar with shrimp cocktail, Caesar salad, Margherita pizza, and short rib mac and cheese. South Pier.
vendors.shortDescription: Bar with pizza, salads, and short rib mac. South Pier.

vendors.name: W.B. Law Coffee
vendors.opinion: Newark-based coffee company. Coffee and pastries. South Pier.
vendors.shortDescription: Coffee and pastries from a Newark company. South Pier.

vendors.name: Zaro's Bakery
vendors.opinion: Off-shoot of Zaro's Family Bakery, a New York-based chain. Central Plaza East.
vendors.shortDescription: Zaro's Family Bakery. Central Plaza East.

terminals.tips: Terminal A has a strong mix: Shake Shack and Bluestone Lane on South Pier, Guy Fieri's on North Pier, Ess-A-Bagel and Carlito's in Hammerhead. Central Plaza Food Court has D'Taco Joint, Mediterranean Grill, Playa Bowls, and Sora.

terminals.description: Newark Terminal B has coffee, sandwiches, pizza, Mexican, Japanese, and wine bars across B1, B2, B3 and food courts.

vendors.name: Belgian Beer Cafe
vendors.opinion: Bottled and draft beer with burgers, beef stew, and bar foods. B2 Rotunda, pre-security.
vendors.shortDescription: Beer, burgers, beef stew. B2 Rotunda, pre-security.

vendors.name: Budweiser Brewhouse
vendors.opinion: Bar for Budweiser fans. B1, pre-security.
vendors.shortDescription: Budweiser bar. B1, pre-security.

vendors.name: Dunkin'
vendors.opinion: Three locations in Terminal B: baggage claim, international arrivals, NEC station.
vendors.shortDescription: Coffee and donuts; three locations in Terminal B.

vendors.name: Firehouse Subs
vendors.opinion: Florida-based sandwich chain. B1 connector, post-security.
vendors.shortDescription: Subs. B1 connector, post-security.

vendors.name: Illy Coffee
vendors.opinion: Coffee, tea, water. B1 connector, B2 satellite.
vendors.shortDescription: Coffee and tea. B1 connector, B2 satellite.

vendors.name: Jersey Reserve
vendors.opinion: Coffee, tea, water. B level 2, international arrivals.
vendors.shortDescription: Coffee and tea. B level 2, international arrivals.

vendors.name: Liberty Eatery
vendors.opinion: Breakfast and Mexican options. Headhouse north.
vendors.shortDescription: Breakfast and Mexican. Headhouse north.

vendors.name: Little Tony's Pizza
vendors.opinion: Slices, calzones, and salads. B1 connector, pre-security.
vendors.shortDescription: Pizza, calzones, salads. B1 connector, pre-security.

vendors.name: Malone's Fish Market
vendors.opinion: Lobster rolls, fish and chips, beer, and cocktails. B1 concourse, post-security.
vendors.shortDescription: Lobster rolls, fish and chips. B1 concourse, post-security.

vendors.name: Panda Express
vendors.opinion: Orange chicken and more. Food hall, pre-security.
vendors.shortDescription: Chinese chain. Food hall, pre-security.

vendors.name: Piattino's
vendors.opinion: Chicken Parmesan and red-sauce dishes. B3.
vendors.shortDescription: Italian with chicken Parm and red-sauce. B3.

vendors.name: Salsarita's Mexican Grill
vendors.opinion: Quesadillas, burritos, and more. B Food court.
vendors.shortDescription: Mexican chain. B Food court.

vendors.name: Smashburger
vendors.opinion: Smashed patties. B Food Court.
vendors.shortDescription: Burger chain. B Food Court.

vendors.name: Sora Japanese Cuisine & Sushi Bar
vendors.opinion: Full-service Japanese with sushi, tempura, and more. B1 rotunda, post-security.
vendors.shortDescription: Full-service Japanese. B1 rotunda, post-security.

vendors.name: Starbucks
vendors.opinion: Two locations in Terminal B: food court pre-security and B1 connector.
vendors.shortDescription: Coffee; two locations in Terminal B.

vendors.name: StrEAT
vendors.opinion: Grab-and-go foods plus coffee and tea. B3 rotunda, post-security.
vendors.shortDescription: Grab-and-go and coffee. B3 rotunda, post-security.

vendors.name: The Unusual Times
vendors.opinion: Roast beef dip sandwiches and pub foods. B2 rotunda, post-security.
vendors.shortDescription: Bar with roast beef dip and pub food. B2 rotunda.

vendors.name: Tony and Benny's
vendors.opinion: Walk-up pizza counter rated by Dave Portnoy. Food court, pre-security.
vendors.shortDescription: Pizza counter. Food court, pre-security.

vendors.name: Vino Volo
vendors.opinion: Wine bar with small plates. B3 Rotunda, post-security.
vendors.shortDescription: Wine bar and small plates. B3 Rotunda, post-security.

vendors.name: Zaro's Bakeshop
vendors.opinion: Zaro's Family Bakery. Food court, pre-security.
vendors.shortDescription: Zaro's bakery. Food court, pre-security.

terminals.tips: Terminal B: Vino Volo and Piattino's for sit-down; Tony and Benny's and Smashburger in the food court. Sora for Japanese. Dunkin' and Starbucks for coffee.

terminals.description: Newark Terminal C is the United hub with the most dining at EWR: Abruzzo steakhouse, Saison, Kaedama, Tsukiji Fishroom, and many more across Gates C70–99, C101–115, and C120–139.

vendors.name: Abruzzo Italian Steakhouse
vendors.opinion: Mario Carbone (Major Food Group) helped open. Chicken Parm, veal chops, steaks. Gates C70–99.
vendors.shortDescription: Italian steakhouse by Major Food Group. Gates C70–99.

vendors.name: Bar Left
vendors.opinion: Cocktail bar with small plates and tablet ordering. Gates C70–99.
vendors.shortDescription: Cocktail bar with tablet ordering. Gates C70–99.

vendors.name: Bar Right
vendors.opinion: Cocktail bar with small plates and tablet ordering. Gates C101–115.
vendors.shortDescription: Cocktail bar. Gates C101–115.

vendors.name: Breakfast Boli
vendors.opinion: Egg-filled breakfast stromboli. Gates C70–99 and C120–139.
vendors.shortDescription: Breakfast stromboli. Gates C70–99 and C120–139.

vendors.name: Caps Beer Garden
vendors.opinion: High-top tables with tablets for ordering beer. Gates C120–139.
vendors.shortDescription: Beer garden with tablet ordering. Gates C120–139.

vendors.name: Casciano's
vendors.opinion: Italian sandwiches from owners of the Grill and Carbone in Manhattan. Gates C70–99.
vendors.shortDescription: Italian sandwiches. Gates C70–99.

vendors.name: CIBO Express Gourmet Market
vendors.opinion: Kosher sandwiches, wraps, grab-and-go. Gate 125 and multiple locations.
vendors.shortDescription: Kosher grab-and-go. Gate 125, multiple locations.

vendors.name: Creperie Juliette
vendors.opinion: Savory and sweet crepes. Concourse C1, food hall west.
vendors.shortDescription: Crepes. Concourse C1, food hall west.

vendors.name: Custom Burger
vendors.opinion: Fast-casual burgers with help from Pat LaFrieda. Gates C70–99 and C120–139.
vendors.shortDescription: Burgers by Pat LaFrieda. Gates C70–99 and C120–139.

vendors.name: Daily
vendors.opinion: Shawarma, scallops, pasta, and more in an open kitchen. Gate C73.
vendors.shortDescription: Open kitchen: shawarma, scallops, pasta. Gate C73.

vendors.name: Dunkin'
vendors.opinion: Two locations: baggage claim pre-security and Gates C101–115 post-security.
vendors.shortDescription: Coffee and donuts; two locations in Terminal C.

vendors.name: Eggy Weggy
vendors.opinion: Breakfast sandwiches and omelets. Gates C70–99 and C120–139.
vendors.shortDescription: Breakfast sandwiches and omelets. Gates C70–99 and C120–139.

vendors.name: Flip Flop Pancakes
vendors.opinion: Pancakes to-go with different flavors. Gates C120–139 and C70–99.
vendors.shortDescription: Pancakes to-go. Gates C120–139 and C70–99.

vendors.name: Flora Cafe
vendors.opinion: Falafel, hummus, baba ghanoush from tablets. Gates C120–139.
vendors.shortDescription: Mediterranean; tablet ordering. Gates C120–139.

vendors.name: Forno Magico
vendors.opinion: Sit-down restaurant with pizza. Gates C120–139.
vendors.shortDescription: Sit-down pizza. Gates C120–139.

vendors.name: Garden State Diner
vendors.opinion: Breakfast all day, soups, salads, sandwiches, full bar. Gates C101–115.
vendors.shortDescription: Diner with breakfast all day and full bar. Gates C101–115.

vendors.name: Happy Clam Oases
vendors.opinion: Chicken Parm sandwiches, clam linguini, red sauce. Gates C101–115.
vendors.shortDescription: Chicken Parm, clam linguini. Gates C101–115.

vendors.name: Kaedama
vendors.opinion: Ramen, dumplings, sushi. Gates C70–99.
vendors.shortDescription: Ramen, dumplings, sushi. Gates C70–99.

vendors.name: Little Purse
vendors.opinion: Dumpling restaurant from Top Chef star Dale Talde. Gates C70–99 and C101–115.
vendors.shortDescription: Dumplings by Dale Talde. Gates C70–99 and C101–115.

vendors.name: Mélange Cafe and Mélange Bakery Chocolatier
vendors.opinion: Jacques Torres chocolates, cookies, alfajores, crepes. Gates C70–99.
vendors.shortDescription: Jacques Torres chocolates and bakery. Gates C70–99.

vendors.name: Notorious PIG
vendors.opinion: Tacos and more for breakfast, lunch, dinner. Gates C70–99.
vendors.shortDescription: Tacos and more. Gates C70–99.

vendors.name: Novella
vendors.opinion: Pizza, pasta, burgers from tablets. Gates C70–99.
vendors.shortDescription: Pizza, pasta, burgers; tablet ordering. Gates C70–99.

vendors.name: Oeno Wine Bar
vendors.opinion: Over 60 wines by the glass and bottle, plus panini. Gates C70–99.
vendors.shortDescription: Wine bar with panini. Gates C70–99.

vendors.name: Philly Cheesesteaks
vendors.opinion: Airport Philly cheesesteaks with different toppings. Gates C70–99 and C120–139.
vendors.shortDescription: Philly cheesesteaks. Gates C70–99 and C120–139.

vendors.name: Poppy's Bagels
vendors.opinion: Bagel sandwiches. Baggage claim and Gate C75, C104.
vendors.shortDescription: Bagel sandwiches. Baggage claim and C75, C104.

vendors.name: Proof Whiskey Bar
vendors.opinion: Bar with lots of whiskeys. Gates C101–115.
vendors.shortDescription: Whiskey bar. Gates C101–115.

vendors.name: Red Mango
vendors.opinion: Frozen yogurt with toppings. Gates C70–99.
vendors.shortDescription: Frozen yogurt. Gates C70–99.

vendors.name: Riviera
vendors.opinion: Burgers, fries, croque madame from tablets. Gates C70–99.
vendors.shortDescription: Burgers, fries, croque madame. Gates C70–99.

vendors.name: Saison
vendors.opinion: Sit-down French by Alain Ducasse. Gates C120–139. Splurge.
vendors.shortDescription: Alain Ducasse French. Gates C120–139.

vendors.name: Starbucks Coffee
vendors.opinion: Several locations: baggage claim and Gates C84, C94, C120–139.
vendors.shortDescription: Coffee; several locations in Terminal C.

vendors.name: Supreme Bowl
vendors.opinion: Bowls. Concourse C1, food hall west.
vendors.shortDescription: Bowls. Concourse C1, food hall west.

vendors.name: Surf Bar
vendors.opinion: Oysters, sushi, and other seafood. Gate C81.
vendors.shortDescription: Oysters, sushi, seafood. Gate C81.

vendors.name: Tagliare
vendors.opinion: Reheated pizza slices. Gates C120–139.
vendors.shortDescription: Pizza slices. Gates C120–139.

vendors.name: The Deli
vendors.opinion: Sandwiches. Gates C120–139 food hall.
vendors.shortDescription: Sandwiches. Gates C120–139 food hall.

vendors.name: The Panini Shop
vendors.opinion: Sandwiches. Concourse C1 near Gate C75.
vendors.shortDescription: Panini and sandwiches. Concourse C1 near C75.

vendors.name: Tsukiji Fishroom
vendors.opinion: Sushi purportedly flown in from Japan's Tsukiji Market. Gates C70–99.
vendors.shortDescription: Sushi from Tsukiji. Gates C70–99.

vendors.name: Uptop Pizza
vendors.opinion: Personal pizzas rated by Dave Portnoy. Baggage claim and Gates C70–99.
vendors.shortDescription: Personal pizzas. Baggage claim and C70–99.

vendors.name: Vanguard Kitchen
vendors.opinion: Sit-down with marbled tabletops and international food. Gates C101–115.
vendors.shortDescription: International food. Gates C101–115.

vendors.name: Vesper Tavern
vendors.opinion: American restaurant with full bar for watching sports. Gates C70–99.
vendors.shortDescription: American with full bar. Gates C70–99.

vendors.name: Wabi Sabi
vendors.opinion: Dumplings, fried crab claws, gyoza. Gates C70–99 and C120–139.
vendors.shortDescription: Dumplings and gyoza. Gates C70–99 and C120–139.

vendors.name: Wanderlust Burger Bar
vendors.opinion: Burger and beer bar from Burger Bash winner Josh Capon. Gates C70–99.
vendors.shortDescription: Burgers and beer by Josh Capon. Gates C70–99.

vendors.name: Wendy's
vendors.opinion: Square burgers, nuggets, fries. Gates C101–115.
vendors.shortDescription: Fast food. Gates C101–115.

vendors.name: World Bean Coffee
vendors.opinion: Coffee chain with baked goods. Baggage claim and Gates C70–99, C120–139.
vendors.shortDescription: Coffee and baked goods. Baggage claim and C70–99, C120–139.

terminals.tips: Terminal C is the United hub with the most options at EWR. Abruzzo and Saison for splurge; Kaedama, Tsukiji Fishroom, and Little Purse for quality. Custom Burger, Wendy's, and Starbucks for speed. Re-upload this file to your DigitalOcean knowledge base after editing.

terminals.description: RDU Terminal 2 has the best dining, including local brands like Crawford's and Bond Brothers.

vendors.name: Crawford's Genuine
vendors.opinion: James Beard–nominated Chef Scott Crawford. Braised short ribs, crispy trout, salads. The best meal at RDU — plan for it.
vendors.shortDescription: Upscale Southern by Chef Scott Crawford; best meal at RDU.

vendors.name: Bond Brothers Kitchen & Bar
vendors.opinion: Cary brewery at the airport. No Turbulence IPA and smoked brisket. Worth the walk to D.
vendors.shortDescription: Local brewery with beer and barbecue near D gates.

vendors.name: Bobby's Burgers
vendors.opinion: Bobby Flay's Crunchburger. Consistently good.
vendors.shortDescription: Bobby Flay's burgers in mid-concourse.

vendors.name: BurgerFi
vendors.opinion: All-natural Angus and Beyond Burger option. Quick and reliable.
vendors.shortDescription: Burgers including Beyond option near D gates.

vendors.name: Half Moon Empanadas
vendors.opinion: Hand-made empanadas. Unique and fast.
vendors.shortDescription: Hand-made empanadas near C1.

vendors.name: La Farm Bakery Café
vendors.opinion: Local bakery. Pastries and sandwiches. Great for breakfast.
vendors.shortDescription: Local bakery and cafe; breakfast and lunch.

vendors.name: Starbucks
vendors.opinion: Coffee and a bite. You know what you're getting.
vendors.shortDescription: Coffee and pastries in mid-concourse.

vendors.name: Jason's Deli
vendors.opinion: Sandwiches and soup. Solid when you're near the D gates.
vendors.shortDescription: Deli sandwiches and soup near D gates.

terminals.tips: Terminal 2 is where to eat at RDU. Crawford's is the standout; Bond Brothers for beer and barbecue. Bobby's Burgers and Half Moon for something quicker.

terminals.description: RDU Terminal 1 has a smaller set of options, mostly quick-serve.

vendors.name: Char-Grill
vendors.opinion: Local burger chain. Quick and classic.
vendors.shortDescription: Local burger chain in RDU T1.

vendors.name: Salsarita's Fresh Cantina
vendors.opinion: Burritos and tacos. Fast and filling.
vendors.shortDescription: Burritos and tacos in T1.

vendors.name: Starbucks
vendors.opinion: Coffee and breakfast. Standard option in T1.
vendors.shortDescription: Coffee and breakfast in T1.

terminals.tips: Terminal 1 is smaller. Char-Grill and Salsarita's are your best bets for a real meal.
